title: Investigate curmudgeon c-cycle carry-over re-verification hygiene: a c4 review can inherit stale c3 hole text that has already been resolved by a separate ISS in the intervening time. Author PROP-NNN proposing a hygiene mechanism (e.g., c-cycle pre-flight that re-validates carry-forward holes against current data state).
scope_authority: Design proposal only. Investigate the pattern, propose a mechanism. Operator + cowork-claude apply after review. Tinker does NOT self-apply prompt edits.

source_signal.verbatim_observation: Side signal: WIN-055.c4 (2026-05-17T22:55Z) inherited stale c3 hole text re: 'See Part 6.13' references that had been removed 29h earlier by ISS-2025; suggest tinker investigate c-cycle carry-over re-verification hygiene as low-priority process improvement.
source_signal.gap_class: analyst-summary-write-gap (PROP-036 family) — the observation was surfaced in latest-analysis-summary.txt but not promoted to a routed inbox item or directive. Cowork-claude/operator filing this directive captures the recommendation properly so it survives summary overwrites.

bug_summary.pattern: When curmudgeon does cycle-N+1 review of a target, it can copy/restate hole text from cycle-N. If the underlying issue documented in cycle-N has been INDEPENDENTLY resolved between c-N and c-N+1 (via a different ISS routed and patched), the c-N+1 review may still document the hole as if it were unresolved — because curmudgeon reads its own prior review file but not the live current data state at the moment of authoring.
bug_summary.concrete_instance: WIN-055.c4 (authored 2026-05-17T22:55Z) carried forward c3 hole text claiming "See Part 6.13" references existed in the WIN-055 prose. ISS-2025 had removed those references 29h earlier (~2026-05-16T18:00Z). The c4 review treated this as a still-open hole. Caught by analyst Mode 1 on 2026-05-18T02:07Z when picking up the EXP for ISS-2103 (which has its own dangling-pointer half).
bug_summary.blast_radius: Soft / process-quality only. The hole gets re-routed; analyst Mode 2b or decider would eventually OBE-dismiss when the carry-forward proves stale. But it costs an EXP-author cycle of wasted analyst-Opus time per occurrence, AND may produce false flags in curmudgeon-side metrics (drift-audit, holes_found counts) that propagate into PROP-030 backlog-trend tier thresholds.
bug_summary.frequency_unknown: N=1 documented observation. Could be rare (cycle-N+1 reviews are infrequent) or could be systemic (every multi-cycle curmudgeon review on a moving target inherits some stale carry-forward).

tasks_for_tinker.description: Grep monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.json for c-cycle pairs where N has holes mentioning ISSs that were closed between c-N and c-N+1 timestamps. Compute frequency: how many c-cycle reviews show the pattern? Is this N=1 anomaly or systemic?
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Survey results in PROP exec_summary, with grep methodology + counts.

tasks_for_tinker.description: If pattern is non-trivial (>3% of multi-cycle reviews), propose a mechanism. Candidates: (a) curmudgeon pre-flight loads list of ISSs closed since prior c-N, checks each prior hole against that list, marks carry-forward holes as OBE-suspect; (b) drift-audit fingerprint check before cycle N+1: did the relevant section content change in a way that would have addressed any prior holes?; (c) decider-side check at intake: filter c-N+1 holes whose text matches text of already-closed ISSs from c-N era. Be opinionated, pick one with counter-arguments.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: phase_1_design section in PROP with mechanism + tradeoffs.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Mandatory counter-arguments per operator preference. Especially: (a) is this just normal review-cycle churn that the system handles correctly via OBE-dismissal downstream? (b) does the proposed mechanism risk over-suppressing legitimate carry-forward holes (an item flagged in c-N AND independently resolved in another ISS still benefits from explicit verification in c-N+1)?

in_scope_for_tinker: Survey the pattern. If N=1 isolated, document and close as no-action.
Propose a mechanism IF pattern is non-trivial.
Mark directive completed via DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception once PROP is filed.
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT self-apply any prompt edit.
Do NOT implement the mechanism — design proposal only.
Do NOT expand into a broader curmudgeon-cycle-hygiene refactor — single specific pattern observation here.
expected_artifacts: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-curmudgeon-c-cycle-carry-over-hygiene.json (status: design-pending-operator-review OR no-action-needed if survey shows N=1 isolated)
related_props: PROP-036 (analyst summary-write canonical fix) — this directive itself is a workaround for the same gap class: analyst surfaced the observation only in summary text, which was about to be overwritten. PROP-036 should ideally have flagged this for routing.
PROP-020 (drift-audit) — the proposed mechanism (b) might reuse drift-audit infrastructure
deadline_target: Next tinker scheduled run (daily 03:30Z tomorrow) or earlier via operator manual trigger. Low priority — no immediate blocker.
